title: Prepare and deploy the EI assessment app to production (“go live”)

requirements.text: Create a production-ready deployment configuration for the existing EI assessment app on the Internet Computer, including building the frontend assets and deploying the Motoko backend canister and frontend canister as a live/production instance.
acceptanceCriteria: A documented set of deployment steps exists (commands and order of operations) to deploy the backend and frontend canisters to a production network target (e.g., IC mainnet).
The deployed app loads successfully in a browser from the production frontend canister URL and can reach the backend canister without CORS/agent errors.
Internet Identity authentication works in production: user can log in, log out, and remain authenticated across refreshes.
A user can complete the 50-item assessment in production, submit it, and then view their computed results (five competency totals + categories).
A surveyor (allowlisted principal) can access the Surveyor Dashboard in production and export submissions to CSV and JSON.
An admin can add/remove surveyor principals in production and the permission change takes effect without redeploying.

requirements.text: Add a production readiness checklist and smoke-test flow to confirm the app is functioning correctly immediately after deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Checklist includes at minimum: canister IDs recorded, frontend URL recorded, Internet Identity login verified, assessment submission verified, results view verified, surveyor export verified, and admin surveyor management verified.
Smoke-test flow can be executed by a developer or operator in under 10 minutes using only the deployed app UI and documented commands (if any).

constraints: Respect the single-actor backend architecture (all Motoko logic in backend/main.mo).
Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui or any paths listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: Adding new assessment questions, changing scoring logic, or modifying interpretation thresholds.
Adding third-party services (analytics, email, external databases, external auth providers).
